items.title: First Nations father teaches dying Indigenous language to sons through bedtime story

items.summary: Patrick and Susan Doyle recently lost almost everything in a house fire, including their beloved dogs, Oscar and Mercy. While the couple escaped with their lives, Mrs Doyle was badly burnt. They are warning others to be better prepared.

items.summary: Back on country, Aunty Polly Cutmore says she feels like a child again when visiting her ancestral lands. Now, as a respected Goombri elder, she shares the stories of her country.

items.summary: Annie Ashton's husband took his own life after suffering from a gambling addiction. Since then, she's campaigned for stronger regulation of the gambling industry and greater awareness of gambling harm.

items.summary: As the social and health benefits of a not-for-profit dance group for over 50s are felt, industry insiders hope more funding will be invested in the arts sector ahead of the Tasmanian state budget.

items.title: Coalition will repeal Labor's property tax changes and spend $22b on income tax cuts
items.summary: The Coalition would repeal all of Labor's negative gearing and capital gains changes and spend $22.5 billion over four years handing back tax "bracket creep" to workers if elected.

items.title: Taylor's bold budget reply a mix of sound and dubious policy commitments
items.summary: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor commits to phasing in income tax indexation under a "tax back guarantee" that he says "will fully protect all taxpayers from inflation".

items.title: Sensory-friendly show gives theatre lovers a more inclusive experience
items.summary: The State Theatre Company South Australia has introduced a sensory-friendly performance for a broader range of audience members including neurodivergent people.

items.title: Sam Kerr to leave Chelsea Football Club after six years
items.summary: The Matildas star has spent over six years playing for the London-based team.

items.title: Mannagh's heavenly night leads Cats to statement win over Lions
items.summary: Geelong records a 41-point win over Brisbane on the back of Shaun Mannagh's five-goal, 30-disposal performance at the Gabba.

items.title: Miraculous try-savers hand NSW second straight Origin series win
items.summary: For the second straight year, NSW secures the Women's State of Origin shield in Game II, with Jesse Southwell and Teagan Berry coming up with stunning try-savers to get the Blues over the line.

items.title: Calls for more education following the suspected death of Mornington Peninsula's beloved 'Sammy' the seal
items.summary: Marine experts are calling for greater education on aquatic life following the death of a seal believed to be known by Mornington Peninsula locals as Sammy.

items.title: Coalition wants to slash migration, but businesses say economy needs skills
items.summary: The Coalition wants to limit Australia's net migration intake to only one person per new home built, but businesses say the economy needs a "sustainable" migration program.

items.title: Bosisto's to triple its eucalyptus oil production with new $10 million distillery in central Victoria
items.summary: A 174-year-old Australian company is trying to address a decline in local eucalyptus oil production by opening a new distillery in central Victoria.

items.title: Illawarra grandmother blames horrific roadside stabbing on Ozempic
items.summary: An Illawarra woman stabbed multiple times by her mother-in-law has told a court she continues to be haunted by images of the roadside attack.

items.title: Who is in President Trump's entourage of billionaires and executives in Beijing?
items.summary: US President Donald Trump has brought a delegation of business executives for his visit to Beijing. Here's why they made the trans-Pacific trip.

items.title: Trump asked about talks with Xi Jinping, Taiwan at Temple of Heaven
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping's meeting in China featured tech titans, Taiwan and flattery
items.summary: From a red carpet welcome to a high-profile tech entourage and a pointed reference to Taiwan, here are the main takeaways from Donald Trump's meeting with Xi Jinping in China.

items.title: Albanese pulls Tammy Tyrrell trick, battlelines drawn in tax fight
items.summary: After failing to catch anyone by surprise with the budget, Anthony Albanese had another trick up his sleeve.

items.title: An Accidental Leader | Rob Armstrong
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Madonna, Shakira and BTS to co-headline World Cup final half-time show
items.summary: The first-ever FIFA World Cup final half-time show has been announced.

items.title: Police mine shaft search fails to find evidence of missing SA woman Trisha Graf
items.summary: Major Crime investigators have packed up after focusing on outback SA mine shafts in their search for 41-year-old Trisha Graf, who went missing last year.

items.title: Ronda Rousey back for redemption as she fights Gina Carano in MMA bout
items.summary: UFC Hall of Famer Ronda Rousey is returning to the ring after almost a decade away, but this time she will be fighting on her own terms.

items.title: 3D-printed guns, taser and ammunition among weapons cache seized in Canberra
items.summary: A police investigation has uncovered a large cache of weapons in the national capital's suburbs, less than a kilometre from the Belconnen Police Station.

items.title: Roads of dead mice as plague blights standards of living in WA towns
items.summary: As locals in a Western Australian town battle a siege of mice, farmers still do not have permission to use a high-strength bait to combat them.

items.title: NSW Ambulance experts quizzed at Clare Nowland taser-death inquest
items.summary: A senior NSW Ambulance officer tells the Clare Nowland inquest that emergency services could have taken more time responding to the 95-year-old's dementia-related aggression.

items.title: Woman dead, man arrested after stabbing in Canberra's inner north
items.summary: Paramedics treated the woman for critical injuries at the scene but she was declared dead on her way to hospital.

items.title: Inquest to be held into disappearance of Queensland man Nathan Brosnan
items.summary: Nathan Brosnan, 35, went missing from Logan while on parole. He is suspected to have died, but his body has never been found.

items.title: NDIS bill creates funding cut powers, civil penalties and stricter test for access
items.summary: Legislation designed to save $35 billion in NDIS spending is introduced to parliament, revealing new powers for the minister to cut funding to classes of supports, automate some decisions and create requirements for prospective participants to exhaust all other treatment options before entering the NDIS.

items.title: Gold Coast Suns coach Damien Hardwick speaks to media in Darwin
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Six passengers from hantavirus-hit cruise ship due to arrive in Perth today
items.summary: Six passengers from a cruise ship at the centre of a deadly hantavirus outbreak are testing negative for the illness and are symptom-free, the federal health minister says, as they prepare to be repatriated to Australia.

items.title: Gold Coast Suns coach Damien Hardwick says Mark Opie will continue duties despite AFL probe
items.summary: Gold Coast Suns coach Damien Hardwick says team manager Mark Opie will continue his match-day duties despite being investigated by the AFL integrity unit over his links to a bookmaking firm.

items.title: Coalition proposes plan to end tax bracket creep
items.summary: The Coalition has made a bold play to win back voters with a plan to automatically index income tax brackets to keep up with inflation.

items.title: Why Gold Coast's retro Chargers jersey is already the toast of the NRL's Magic Round
items.summary: One of the best parts of Magic Round is going jersey-spotting but before a ball is kicked in Brisbane the Titans have already cleaned up via their Chargers-inspired retro strip.

items.title: Coles has been found to have misled shoppers. These charts show how
items.summary: The pricing patterns of these six products show how Coles was found to have misled consumers through its Down Down pricing patterns.

items.title: Newcastle Waters cattle station granted permit to cull 20,000 native birds
items.summary: A permit to cull 20,000 native birds has been granted to a cattle station in the Northern Territory in response to an "overpopulation" of destructive and "exceptionally noisy" galahs and little corellas.

items.title: How to clean your sneakers in a washing machine
items.summary: Experts reveal their tips on bringing your favourite sneakers back to life at home.

items.title: Scottish Premier League title race goes to final day with Hearts leading Celtic by one point
items.summary: Celtic's Kelechi Iheanacho scores a controversial penalty with the last kick of the game to secure a 3-2 win at Motherwell, sending the Scottish Premiership title race to a final-day showdown against leaders Heart of Midlothian.

items.title: More than 800 northern corroboree frogs released into the wild in southern NSW to bolster numbers
items.summary: More than 800 tiny, technicoloured northern corroboree frogs are released into the wild to try to bolster the numbers of one of Australia's most critically endangered amphibians.

items.title: Coles found to have misled shoppers on discounted items in bombshell Federal Court case
items.summary: Supermarket giant Coles broke consumer law by misleading shoppers on discount prices, a Federal Court judge has found.

items.title: Coalition to bar non-citizens from welfare, NDIS under fresh migration policy
items.summary: Non-citizens, including permanent residents, will be barred from accessing welfare under an updated migration crackdown promised by the Coalition.

items.title: Palaszczuk partner Reza Adib's first court hearing for rape charges
items.summary: A court hearing has been held for Vahid Reza Adib, the partner of former Queensland premier Annastacia Palaszczuk, for the first time since he was charged with rape, deprivation of liberty and sexual assault earlier this month.

items.title: Federal Court dismisses legal action over Murray flood plain restoration
items.summary: A community group's argument that a delegate to the federal water minister made a legal error by approving the Nyah Floodplain Restoration Project has been rejected.

items.title: SA Police says fire at North Adelaide Golf Course suspicious
items.summary: Police are investigating a suspicious fire that destroyed a temporary structure at the course amid a controversial redevelopment. In a separate incident, work was briefly halted when the perimeter fence was breached, and two people were later arrested for alleged trespassing.

items.title: How will the NT amend child protection laws? Will the Aboriginal Child Placement Principle change?
items.summary: Here's what you need to know about the proposed changes to the NT child protection legislation, introduced into parliament on Wednesday.

items.title: Eddie Jones suspended by Japan rugby for verbal abuse on Australian tour
items.summary: Eddie Jones has been suspended from coaching Japan during its rugby union Test match against Italy in July after "incidences of verbal abuse" during a recent tour of Australia.

items.title: Dennington Dogs forfeit remainder of men's senior Warrnambool District league season
items.summary: A country Victorian football team that was beaten by a collective 1,101 points in four matches forfeits the remainder of the senior season.

items.title: Prosecutors to seek new trial after Alex Murdaugh murder convictions overturned
items.summary: An infamous case will be retried after a US court ruled in favour of the South Carolina father.

items.title: Greenbushes residents say Talison's free car wash plan misses the mark
items.summary: Residents plagued by dust from Australia's oldest and largest lithium mine say a plan for a free car wash by the mine's owner misses the mark.

items.title: Public memorial for 702 ABC Sydney presenter James Valentine to be held at Town Hall
items.summary: Sydney Town Hall will host a memorial for the late radio presenter and musician, with limited spots available for members of the public.

items.title: Drug counsellor Erik Fleming sentenced for role in supplying ketamine to Matthew Perry
items.summary: The man who delivered Friends star Matthew Perry the doses of ketamine that killed him says he is haunted by the mistakes he made.

items.title: Teenager charged over home invasion linked to Melbourne arson attacks
items.summary: Police say a shot was fired by intruders during an aggravated home invasion earlier this month, which is being linked to a series of arson attacks on licensed venues in Melbourne.

items.title: New virus to control surging feral rabbit population at least 10 years away
items.summary: Feral rabbits are causing damage on farms and in towns, but biological control research has been delayed by funding woes.

items.title: Surfside residents fear homes could be lost to sea in next storm
items.summary: Residents living on an idyllic stretch of the NSW far south coast are demanding action to protect their homes from erosion.

items.title: Michael Voss exit closes door on Carlton's era of premiership contention that never was
items.summary: Michael Voss leaves Carlton having taken the club to places it has rarely been in a quarter of a century, but still adrift from where it ultimately wants to be.

items.title: Gun shop owners grapple with uncertainty amid firearm reform
items.summary: A lack of clarity on the South Australian government's position on gun control reform has caused a downturn in revenue for retailers.

items.title: Mixed reaction from Australia's diaspora communities to federal budget's housing reform
items.summary: Housing policy changes announced in the federal budget could make it easier for some to buy homes, but others are planning to sell before they lose too much.

items.title: Former Australia Test cricketer Marcus North named England selector
items.summary: The appointment of its first Australian selector, its first female coach and a new-look team has made it a landmark day for England's men's cricket team as it prepares to plunge into its post-Ashes Test recovery.

items.title: TV tower shutdown prompts concern about reliance on foreign satellites
items.summary: Phone, internet and council-run TV services have been making way for foreign satellite technology in rural Queensland as ground infrastructure ages, forcing councils to shut down services.

items.title: How a sister's love unraveled the tragic death of Olympic hopeful Ian Walker and unmasked the teacher who abused him
items.summary: Ian Walker was one of the most promising athletes in the country until his life started to unravel, and the search for the truth in his tragic story has finally come to light.

items.title: Australians 'literally dying' as aged care average wait times surpass 365 days
items.summary: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has conceded the state of aged care in Australia "is not good enough" after a new report revealed people are waiting an average of a year for services.

items.title: Agnes Water accommodation provider fined after refusing to allow woman to stay with guide dog
items.summary: A Queensland accommodation provider has been the first to be successful convicted under the state's Guide, Hearing and Assistance Dogs (GHAD) Act.

items.title: Igor Arrieta wins wild Giro d'Italia stage, despite crashing and taking a wrong turn
items.summary: The Giro d'Italia treated supporters to one of the most astonishing finishes to a bike race in history when Igor Arrieta had to twice regather himself after first crashing and later taking a wrong turn to claim a stunning victory.

items.title: 'From Farage to Farrer': Rising populist tide makes Labor bolder
items.summary: If this week's budget marked the start of the Albanese government's campaign for a third term in office, tonight's budget reply marks Angus Taylor's pitch to prevent a Liberal wipe-out.

items.title: Economists divided on extent of property price hit from budget tax overhaul
items.summary: Some economists are forecasting price falls of up to 5 per cent as a result of measures to reduce intergenerational housing inequality.

items.title: Anne Hathaway and Michaela Coel will bewitch you in Mother Mary
items.summary: Director David Lowery has crafted a dark and mysterious chamber piece that turns a pop star's spiralling crisis into something supernatural.

items.title: How Hollywood became a new home for classical music’s grandest ideas
items.summary: From 19th-century concert traditions to Hollywood’s Golden Age, and back to the concert hall, movies are helping audiences experience live orchestral music.

items.title: It's the ABC More or Less quiz: Eurovision edition
items.summary: We've put down our tiny flags and picked up our record books to challenge what you think you might know about Eurovision.

items.title: Reform UK leader Nigel Farage facing inquiry over $9m gift from crypto billionaire
items.summary: Britain's parliamentary watchdog will begin an investigation into whether Reform UK leader Nigel Farage breached its rules by accepting a 5 million pound ($9.31 million) gift that he did not declare.

items.title: Australia secures aircraft to bring passengers home from hantavirus-hit cruise ship
items.summary: A suitable aircraft to bring home passengers from the hantavirus-hit cruise has been secured and will repatriate the group of six later this week.
